_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to identify and explain how literary devices create layers of meaning and aesthetic qualities in texts — including in poetry.

_source.w: We are learning to identify and explain how literary devices create layers of meaning and aesthetic qualities in texts — including in poetry.
y: Literary devices create the depth and beauty that distinguish literature from ordinary communication.
Understanding how layers of meaning work helps us interpret texts at multiple levels.
Appreciating aesthetic qualities builds our love of language and literature.
eg: We analyse how metaphor creates meaning on both a literal and symbolic level.
We explain how sound devices in poetry — like assonance, consonance and meter — create aesthetic effects.
We discuss how imagery appeals to the senses and creates vivid experiences for readers.
We identify layers of meaning in a poem — the surface narrative and the deeper themes.
We compare how two poets use the same device to create different effects.
We experiment with literary devices in our own poetry to create layers of meaning.
